---
title: "Linear Algebra"
description: "Core linear algebra for research-facing work in CS, AI, and engineering."
page-type: module-overview
status: published
update-status: current
level: foundation
topic: linear-algebra
prerequisites:
  - /start-here/index.qmd
learning-goals:
  - "Build geometric and operator intuition."
  - "Connect core matrix ideas to estimation, optimization, and spectral methods."
  - "Use the module as a bridge into advanced theory papers."
applications:
  - machine-learning
  - optimization
  - signal-processing
keywords:
  - linear algebra
  - least squares
  - spectral methods
maintainer: mtuann
last-reviewed: 2026-04-24
source-check-date: 2026-04-24
review-cadence: annual
date-modified: last-modified
draft: false
---

## Why This Module Matters

Linear algebra is one of the most important modules on the whole site. Modern ML, optimization, signal processing, control, and statistics all keep returning to vectors, operators, geometry, and spectra.

::: {.meta-strip}
::: {.meta-box}
**Prerequisites**
Algebra and late single-variable calculus
:::
::: {.meta-box}
**Unlocks**
Multivariable calculus, optimization, numerics, matrix analysis
:::
::: {.meta-box}
**Research Use**
Least squares, SVD, covariance, kernels, spectral arguments
:::
:::

## Starter Sequence

1. [Vectors and Linear Combinations](concepts/vectors-and-linear-combinations.qmd)
2. Matrices and linear maps
3. Subspaces, basis, dimension
4. [Orthogonality and Least Squares](concepts/orthogonality-and-least-squares.qmd)
5. Eigenvalues and diagonalization
6. [SVD and Low-Rank Approximation](concepts/svd-and-low-rank-approximation.qmd)

## Navigation Rule

The global sidebar shows only the linear algebra `spine`:

- the module overview
- the core concept pages in the main study order

The proof, exercise, application, lab, paper-lab, research, and source-guide pages are `deep-dive companions`. They stay linked from this module page and the topic concept pages, rather than all appearing in the main sidebar at once.

## Deep-Dive Topic Packages

The first two full topic packages in this module are:

- `orthogonality-and-least-squares`
- `svd-and-low-rank-approximation`

If you want to see the whole site model in one place, use this sequence:

1. [Orthogonality and Least Squares](concepts/orthogonality-and-least-squares.qmd)
2. [Projection Theorem and Normal Equations](proofs/projection-theorem-and-normal-equations.qmd)
3. [Exercises: Orthogonality and Least Squares](exercises/orthogonality-and-least-squares.qmd)
4. [Linear Regression Through Projection](applications/linear-regression-through-projection.qmd)
5. [Computation Lab: Projection Geometry and Regression Residuals](labs/orthogonality-and-least-squares-geometry.qmd)
6. [Paper Lab: Randomized Sketching for Least Squares](paper-lab/randomized-sketching-for-least-squares.qmd)
7. [Research Direction: Least Squares, Sketching, and Overparameterized Regression](research/least-squares-sketching-and-overparameterized-regression.qmd)
8. [Source Guide: Orthogonality and Least Squares](sources/orthogonality-and-least-squares.qmd)

For the spectral side of the module, use this second package:

1. [SVD and Low-Rank Approximation](concepts/svd-and-low-rank-approximation.qmd)
2. [Eckart-Young and Truncated SVD](proofs/eckart-young-and-truncated-svd.qmd)
3. [Exercises: SVD and Low-Rank Approximation](exercises/svd-and-low-rank-approximation.qmd)
4. [PCA Through SVD](applications/pca-through-svd.qmd)
5. [Computation Lab: Rank-1 Approximation and PCA Geometry](labs/rank-one-approximation-and-pca-geometry.qmd)
6. [Paper Lab: Finding Structure with Randomness](paper-lab/finding-structure-with-randomness.qmd)
7. [Research Direction: Low-Rank Structure from Randomized Algorithms to Model Adaptation](research/low-rank-structure-from-randomized-algorithms-to-model-adaptation.qmd)
8. [Source Guide: SVD and Low-Rank Approximation](sources/svd-and-low-rank-approximation.qmd)

## What You Want By The End

- geometric intuition
- symbolic fluency
- operator viewpoint
- ability to recognize where a paper is really using spectral structure

## Application Door

If you want a first application target, start with `least squares -> SVD -> PCA -> low-rank approximation`. That chain alone will pay off again and again.

## Sources and Further Reading

- [MIT 18.06SC Linear Algebra resource index](https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/18-06sc-linear-algebra-fall-2011/pages/resource-index/) - `First pass` - strong official module spine. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [Stanford Math 51](https://web.stanford.edu/class/math51/) - `First pass` - current applied framing for the linear algebra side of the module. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [Hefferon, Linear Algebra](https://hefferon.net/linearalgebra/) - `Second pass` - proof-and-exercise depth for self-study. Checked `2026-04-24`.
